# qbuem-json — Complete Reference for AI Agents

> qbuem-json is a high-performance, header-only C++20 JSON library with dual-engine architecture.
> GitHub: https://github.com/qbuem/qbuem-json
> Docs: https://qbuem.com/qbuem-json/
> Version: 1.0.7 · License: Apache 2.0

---

## Overview

qbuem-json provides two complementary JSON engines in a single header file (`qbuem_json.hpp`):

- **DOM engine** — flat-tape architecture, SIMD-accelerated (AVX-512 on x86, NEON on ARM), single arena allocation. Best for exploratory access, dynamic keys, partial reads, large files. Up to 2.9 GB/s parsing, 7.2 GB/s serialization.
- **Nexus Fusion engine** — zero-tape, compile-time FNV-1a dispatch that maps JSON directly into C++ structs. Best for latency-critical micro-DTOs, HFT tick data, embedded. 50–230 ns struct mapping.

**Requirements:** C++20, GCC 13+ or Clang 18+ or Apple Clang.
**Supported platforms:** Linux x86_64, Linux aarch64, macOS Apple Silicon. Windows is NOT supported.
**Dependencies:** Zero. Single header file.

---

## Installation

### Option A: Single Header Drop-in (Recommended)

```bash
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qbuem/qbuem-json/main/include/qbuem_json/qbuem_json.hpp
```

```cpp
#include "qbuem_json.hpp"
```

Compile with:
```bash
g++ -std=c++20 -O3 -march=native my_app.cpp -o my_app
```

### Option B: CMake FetchContent

```cmake
include(FetchContent)
FetchContent_Declare(
    qbuem_json
    GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/qbuem/qbuem-json
    GIT_TAG        v1.0.7
)
FetchContent_MakeAvailable(qbuem_json)
target_link_libraries(your_target PRIVATE qbuem_json::qbuem_json)
```

### Option C: Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/qbuem/qbuem-json.git
cd qbuem-json
cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DQBUEM_JSON_BUILD_TESTS=ON
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure
cmake --install build --prefix /usr/local
```

---

## Quick Start — DOM Engine

```cpp
#include <qbuem_json/qbuem_json.hpp>

int main() {
    std::string json = R"({"name": "Alice", "age": 30, "active": true})";

    qbuem::Document doc;
    qbuem::Value root = qbuem::parse(doc, json);

    std::string name = root["name"].as<std::string>(); // "Alice"
    int         age  = root["age"].as<int>();           // 30
    bool        flag = root["active"].as<bool>();       // true

    // Zero-copy string view (valid while doc is alive)
    std::string_view sv = root["name"].as<std::string_view>();
}
```

## Quick Start — Nexus Fusion Engine (Zero-Tape)

```cpp
#include <qbuem_json/qbuem_json.hpp>

struct Player {
    std::string name;
    int         score = 0;
};
QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS(Player, name, score)  // MUST be outside struct, at namespace scope

int main() {
    // Zero intermediate tape — direct stream-to-struct mapping
    auto player = qbuem::fuse<Player>(R"({"name":"Bob","score":99})");

    // Serialize back to JSON
    std::string json = qbuem::write(player);  // {"name":"Bob","score":99}
}
```

---

## Parsing API

### Parse Functions

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `qbuem::parse(doc, json)` | Standard parse. Builds flat tape. Returns `Value`. Throws on error. |
| `qbuem::parse_reuse(doc, json)` | Same as `parse()` but documents intent to reuse the doc in a hot loop (tape memory is reused, not reallocated). |
| `qbuem::parse_strict(doc, json)` | RFC 8259 strict mode. Throws on trailing commas, leading zeros, lone surrogates. |
| `qbuem::read<T>(json)` | Parse JSON and deserialize into type `T`. Uses DOM engine + type mapping. |
| `qbuem::fuse<T>(json)` | Nexus Fusion: zero-tape direct struct mapping. Fastest path. Requires `QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS`. |

**Important:** `Document` must outlive all `Value` objects derived from it. Values are 16-byte handles pointing into the document's tape.

```cpp
// Hot loop pattern — reuse tape memory
qbuem::Document doc;
doc.reserve(4 * 1024);  // pre-warm to avoid first-parse realloc

while (std::getline(stream, line)) {
    qbuem::Value root = qbuem::parse_reuse(doc, line);
    if (!root.is_valid()) continue;
    process(root);
}
```

### Value Access

```cpp
qbuem::Document doc;
auto root = qbuem::parse(doc, R"({"id": 101, "score": 9.87, "tag": "vip", "active": true})");

// Throwing access — use when you trust the schema
int64_t     id     = root["id"].as<int64_t>();
double      score  = root["score"].as<double>();
std::string tag    = root["tag"].as<std::string>();
bool        active = root["active"].as<bool>();

// Non-throwing — returns std::optional<T>
std::optional<int> id_opt = root["id"].try_as<int>();

// Pipe fallback — never throws, returns default if missing or wrong type
int      id2   = root["id"]     | -1;
double   sc2   = root["score"]  | 0.0;
bool     flag2 = root["active"] | false;

// Safe monadic chain — propagates absent state
std::string city = root.get("user").get("profile").get("city").value_or("Unknown");

// Access with default
int timeout = root.value("timeout", 5000);
```

### Type Checkers (never throw)

```cpp
v.is_valid();    // false if key doesn't exist
v.is_object();
v.is_array();
v.is_string();
v.is_int();
v.is_double();
v.is_bool();
v.is_null();
v.is_number();   // is_int() || is_double()
std::string_view tname = v.type_name();  // "int", "string", "array", etc.
```

### Object Access

```cpp
if (auto config = root.find("config")) {   // returns std::optional<Value>
    int timeout = config->value("timeout", 5000);
}
if (root.contains("optional_field")) {
    process(root["optional_field"]);
}
```

### Array and Object Iteration

```cpp
// Object key-value pairs (C++20 structured bindings)
for (auto [key, val] : root.items()) { /* ... */ }
for (std::string_view key : root.keys()) { /* ... */ }
for (auto val : root.values()) { /* ... */ }

// Array elements
for (auto elem : root["scores"].elements()) {
    int s = elem.as<int>();
}

// Typed array view — zero allocation
for (int score : root["scores"].as_array<int>()) { /* ... */ }

// C++20 ranges
auto high = root["scores"].elements()
    | std::views::filter([](auto v){ return v.as<int>() >= 90; });
```

### JSON Pointer (RFC 6901)

```cpp
// Runtime JSON Pointer
std::string t = root.at("/store/book/0/title").as<std::string>();

// Compile-time JSON Pointer
using namespace qbuem::literals;
auto title = root.at("/store/book/1/title"_jptr).as<std::string>();
```

---

## Struct Mapping — QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS Macro

The `QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS` macro must be placed **outside** the struct, at namespace scope. It generates free functions for ADL-based serialization/deserialization.

```cpp
struct Address {
    std::string street;
    std::string city;
    std::string country;
};
QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS(Address, street, city, country)

struct User {
    uint64_t    id;
    std::string username;
    Address     address;                         // nested struct — works automatically
    std::vector<std::string> tags;               // STL container — works automatically
    std::optional<double>    score;              // optional — maps to null when empty
    bool        active = true;                   // default values are preserved
};
QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS(User, id, username, address, tags, score, active)

// Deserialize
User u = qbuem::read<User>(json_str);      // DOM engine path
User u2 = qbuem::fuse<User>(json_str);    // Nexus Fusion path (zero-tape, fastest)

// Serialize
std::string json    = qbuem::write(u);      // compact
std::string pretty  = qbuem::write(u, 2);   // 2-space indented
```

**WRONG — placing QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS inside the struct:**
```cpp
struct User {
    int id;
    QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS(User, id)  // ❌ compile error — ADL breaks
};
```

**CORRECT — at namespace scope:**
```cpp
struct User { int id; };
QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS(User, id)  // ✅
```

Supports up to 32 fields. For more, use manual ADL hooks (`nexus_pulse`, `from_qbuem_json`, `to_qbuem_json`).

---

## STL Type Mapping

| C++ Type | JSON |
|---|---|
| `std::vector<T>`, `std::list`, `std::deque` | Array `[...]` |
| `std::array<T, N>` | Fixed array |
| `std::set<T>`, `std::unordered_set<T>` | Sorted array |
| `std::map<string, T>`, `std::unordered_map<string, T>` | Object `{...}` |
| `std::optional<T>` | Value or `null` |
| `std::tuple<T...>` | Heterogeneous array |
| `std::pair<T1, T2>` | 2-element array |
| `std::variant<T...>` | Dynamic value match |

All mappings are fully recursive. Example:
```cpp
auto data = qbuem::read<std::map<std::string, std::vector<int>>>(
    R"({"scores": [95, 87, 100], "ids": [1, 2]})"
);
```

---

## Serialization

```cpp
// dump() — re-serialize a parsed Value (includes mutations)
qbuem::Document doc;
auto root = qbuem::parse(doc, R"({"user": {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}})");
root["user"]["age"] = 26;
std::string json   = root.dump();        // compact
std::string pretty = root.dump(2);       // 2-space pretty-print
std::string sub    = root["user"].dump(); // serialize subtree only

// write() — serialize a C++ object
struct Order { int id; double price; std::string symbol; };
QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS(Order, id, price, symbol)
Order o{42, 99.5, "AAPL"};
std::string json2   = qbuem::write(o);       // compact
std::string pretty2 = qbuem::write(o, 2);    // indented

// write_to() — append into a reusable buffer (avoids allocation)
std::string buf;
buf.reserve(4096);
qbuem::write_to(buf, my_struct);
buf.clear();
root.dump(buf);  // dump also accepts a buffer ref
```

---

## Mutations (Non-Destructive Overlay)

The original tape is immutable. Mutations are stored in a fast overlay map.

```cpp
qbuem::Document doc;
auto root = qbuem::parse(doc, R"({"user": {"id": 1, "name": "Alice"}})");

// Scalar mutation
root["user"]["id"]   = 99;
root["user"]["name"] = "Bob";
root["user"]["active"] = true;  // new field
root["user"]["extra"] = nullptr;

// Restore original
root["user"]["id"].unset();  // reverts to original tape value (not null)

// Structural mutations
root.insert("version", 2);
root.insert("label", std::string_view{"preview"});
root.insert_json("meta", R"({"build": "release"})");
root.erase("deprecated_field");
root["tags"].push_back(std::string_view{"simd"});
root["tags"].push_back_json(R"({"nested": "value"})");
root["tags"].erase(0u);  // remove by index (accepts size_t or unsigned int)

// RFC 7396 merge patch
root.merge_patch(R"({"b": 99, "c": null, "d": "new"})");
// c is removed (null in patch), b is updated, d is added
```

---

## Error Handling

```cpp
// Strategy 1: Exceptions (for trusted schemas)
try {
    qbuem::Document doc;
    auto root = qbuem::parse(doc, json);
    int id = root["user"]["id"].as<int>();  // throws if missing or wrong type
} catch (const std::runtime_error& e) {
    std::cerr << "JSON error: " << e.what() << "\n";
}

// Strategy 2: Monadic chain (no throw, for untrusted data)
int timeout = root.get("config").get("server").get("timeout_ms")
    .value_or(5000);  // returns 5000 if any key is missing

// Strategy 3: Boolean checks
if (root["count"].is_int()) {
    process(root["count"].as<int>());
}

// Strategy 4: Pipe fallback (safest, most ergonomic)
int id = root["id"] | -1;
```

---

## Third-Party Types (ADL Hooks)

For types you can't modify (e.g., from glm):

```cpp
namespace glm {
    inline void from_qbuem_json(const qbuem::Value& v, vec3& out) {
        out.x = v[0u].as<float>();
        out.y = v[1u].as<float>();
        out.z = v[2u].as<float>();
    }
    inline void to_qbuem_json(qbuem::Value& root, const vec3& in) {
        root = qbuem::Value::array();
        root.push_back(qbuem::Value(in.x));
        root.push_back(qbuem::Value(in.y));
        root.push_back(qbuem::Value(in.z));
    }
}
glm::vec3 pos = qbuem::read<glm::vec3>("[1.0, 2.0, 3.5]");
```

For Nexus Fusion with third-party types, define `nexus_pulse` in the same namespace:

```cpp
namespace third_party {
    struct Custom { int x; };
    inline void nexus_pulse(std::string_view key, const char*& p, const char* end, Custom& obj) {
        switch (qbuem::json::detail::fnv1a_hash(key)) {
            case qbuem::json::detail::fnv1a_hash_ce("x"):
                qbuem::json::detail::from_json_direct(p, end, obj.x);
                break;
            default:
                qbuem::json::detail::skip_direct(p, end);
                break;
        }
    }
}
```

---

## JSON Patch (RFC 6902)

```cpp
// Apply a JSON Patch with transactional rollback
qbuem::Document doc;
auto root = qbuem::parse(doc, R"({"a": 1, "b": {"c": 2}})");

std::string patch = R"([
    {"op": "replace", "path": "/a", "value": 99},
    {"op": "add",     "path": "/d", "value": "new"},
    {"op": "remove",  "path": "/b/c"}
])";
root.apply_patch(patch);
```

---

## Performance Reference

| Benchmark (twitter.json, 617 KB) | DOM Parsing | Serialization |
|---|---|---|
| Linux x86_64 GCC 13 (AVX-512) | 2.9 GB/s | 5.3 GB/s |
| Linux aarch64 GCC 14 (NEON) | 2.4 GB/s | 6.1 GB/s |
| macOS Apple Silicon (NEON) | 2.5 GB/s | 7.2 GB/s |

Nexus Fusion: 50–230 ns for direct struct mapping (zero tape).

Competitors (same benchmark): simdjson ~2.5 GB/s parse, yyjson ~2.3 GB/s, RapidJSON ~0.8 GB/s, nlohmann ~0.3 GB/s.

---

## Correctness & Safety

- RFC 8259 (JSON), RFC 6901 (JSON Pointer), RFC 6902 (JSON Patch) compliant
- IEEE 754 round-trip: `parse(serialize(x)) == x` for all finite doubles
- Three-stage float parsing: Eisel-Lemire (~98.8%) → Russ Cox Unrounded Scaling (~1.2%) → strtod (subnormals <0.01%)
- Float serialization: Schubfach (Giulietti 2020) — shortest decimal, no trailing zeros
- 521 passing tests across 20 files (5,556 lines of test code)
- 11 libFuzzer targets
- ASan, UBSan, TSan on every commit

---

## Platform & Compiler Matrix

| Platform | Compiler | SIMD |
|---|---|---|
| Linux x86_64 | GCC 13+, Clang 18+ | AVX-512 / SSE4.2 |
| Linux aarch64 | GCC 14+, Clang 18+ | ARM NEON |
| macOS Apple Silicon | Apple Clang | ARM NEON |

Build flags for maximum performance:
- GCC/Clang: `-std=c++20 -O3 -march=native`
- Apple Silicon: `-std=c++20 -O3 -mcpu=apple-m1`

---

## Common Pitfalls

1. **Document must outlive Values** — `Value` is a 16-byte handle into the tape. Store `Document` and `Value` together (e.g., in a struct).
2. **QBUEM_JSON_FIELDS must be at namespace scope** — placing it inside the struct body breaks ADL.
3. **Use `parse_reuse()` for hot loops** — reuses allocated tape memory without reallocation.
4. **`SafeValue::size()` vs `SafeValue->size()`** — always call `.size()` directly on `SafeValue` (without `->`), which safely returns 0 if the value is absent.
5. **`unset()` restores original tape value** — it does not set to null; it removes the mutation overlay.
6. **Array indices accept plain `int`** — no cast to `size_t` needed; negative indices return invalid `Value{}` safely.

---
